nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Burgh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from one or other of the ten English places named Burgh in northern England and East Anglia, or the six named Brough in Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, Westmorland, and Yorkshire. All these are from Old English burg, burh, Middle English burgh ‘massive earthwork, fortification; manor, borough’. Compare Bury . The placename Burgh is pronounced as borough. The surname Brough arose equally from places now called Burgh and Brough, and is the more common form of the surname in modern Britain, where it is pronounced bruff or broof (/brʊf/). The Scottish surname was probably brought in from Yorkshire. For the surname in Ireland see Burke .

variants.name: Burke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Siobhan, Aileen, Brennan, Brigid, Donovan, Liam, Murphy, Ulick, Aidan, Dermot.

English and Irish (of Anglo-Norman origin): habitational name from Burgh (Castle) in Suffolk, England. Burk represents an Anglo-Norman pronunciation of Old English burg, Middle English burgh ‘fortification’ (see Burgh ).

variants.name: Bury
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Bury in Lancashire (now part of Greater Manchester), or from some other similarly named place. The placename comes from the dative case, byrig, of Old English burh ‘fortified place’.

Walloon and French: habitational name from any of several places so named, in Wallonia (Belgium) and Oise (France). The placename is from Buriacum, the name of a Gallo-Roman estate, composed of the personal name Burius + the locative suffix -acum. This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium.

variants.name: Brough
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from any of various English places called Brough or Burgh. See   Burgh  .
Scottish: habitational name from Brough in Harray (Orkney).
Americanized form of German   Brauch   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Burrow
meanings: topographic name for someone who lived by a castle or manor house, or in a town, especially a chartered town or borough (Middle English burgh, Old English burg ‘fortified place, borough, manor’), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named because it was a fortified town or near an ancient fort, such as Burrow (Leicestershire, Lancashire), Burrough Green (Cambridgeshire), Brough (numerous counties), and Peterborough (Northamptonshire); see also Burgh .

topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or near a mound (Middle English bergh, Old English beorg), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named, such as Burrow (Devon, Somerset). Middle English bergh became throughly confused with burgh in 1 above. Compare Burrough .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: John De Burgh was born in 1190, in Surrey, England, United Kingdom. He married Amabilia De Lindsay in 1217, in Bramber Castle, Sussex, England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died in 1275, in Walkern, Hertfordshire, England, at the age of 85.

fr.lifeSummary: John De Burgh est né en 1190, à Surrey, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il a épousé Amabilia De Lindsay en 1217, à Bramber Castle, Sussex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1275, à Walkern, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 85 ans.

de.lifeSummary: John De Burgh wurde 1190 in Surrey, England, United Kingdom geboren. Er hat 1217 in Bramber Castle, Sussex, England Amabilia De Lindsay geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1275 im Alter von 85 Jahren in Walkern, Hertfordshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: John De Burgh è nato il nel 1190 nel Surrey, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lui sposato Amabilia De Lindsay nel 1217, nel Bramber Castle, Sussex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1275, nel Walkern, Hertfordshire, England, all'età di 85 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: John De Burghは1190年にイギリスイギリススリーで生まれた。1217年にイギリススセックスブランバー カストルでAmabilia De Lindsayと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は1275年にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアウォーケルンで85歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: John De Burgh(은)는 1190년 영국 잉글랜드 수리에서 태어났다. 그는 1217년 영국 수세스 브램버 카스틀에서 Amabilia De Lindsay(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1275년 영국 허트퍼드실르 워커에서 85세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: John De Burgh nasceu em 1190, em Surrey, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele casou-se com Amabilia De Lindsay em 1217, em Bramber Castle, Sussex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1275, em Walkern, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, com 85 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: John De Burgh родился в 1190 г. в местности сурри, Англия, Великобритания. Он женился на женщине по имени Amabilia De Lindsay в 1217 г. в местности брэмбир кастл, суссекс, Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в 1275 г. в местности валкирн, хиртфордшир, Англия в возрасте 85 лет.

es.lifeSummary: John De Burgh nació en 1190, en Surrey, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Se casó con Amabilia De Lindsay en 1217, en Bramber Castle, Sussex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1275, en Walkern, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 85 años.
